NORTHROOT
ProductSystemsDocsJournalExamples

Section

ProductSystemsDocsJournalExamples
Architecture

Trust Kernel Architecture

How records, receipts, journal storage, and offline verification compose.

Northroot is organized as a minimal trust kernel. The kernel does not need one universal runtime to be useful.

Architecture

external runtime
  -> record JSON / JSONL
  -> canonical bytes
  -> hashes and event identity
  -> receipt bound to evidence
  -> optional append-only journal
  -> offline verification without a service dependency

Implementation Notes

Domain-specific event types, authorization, reconciliation, execution lifecycle, and semantic verification are external layers. They may consume the kernel, but they do not become the kernel.

Operational Lessons

Different runtimes can share evidence when the underlying records use stable hashes, explicit receipts, and replayable journals.

Source

northroot/docs/developer/architecture.mdnorthroot/docs/reference/spec.md

Explanations live here. Canonical contracts remain in source.
